meta.name: Herb Flatbread
meta.subtitle: Soft skillet flatbread with garlic, herbs, and olive oil (Herb & Garlic style)

source.name: ssa.cuisines.herb_garlic
notes: Canonical SSA Herb & Garlic baseline flatbread.
Designed as a quick yeast flatbread with deterministic timing bands and a no-yeast fallback variation.

ingredients.label: All-purpose flour
notes: Spoon-and-level for accuracy.
Add extra 1–3 tbsp as needed depending on humidity.

ingredients.label: Instant yeast
notes: 1 packet (about 7 g).

ingredients.label: Sugar (or honey)
notes: Helps yeast wake up and improves browning.

ingredients.label: Plain yogurt (Greek or regular)
notes: Yogurt gives softness and slight tang.
Substitute: sour cream.

ingredients.label: Warm water
notes: Warm, not hot (about 100–110°F).

ingredients.label: Extra-virgin olive oil
notes: Used in dough and for finishing.

ingredients.label: Garlic, finely grated or minced
notes: Reserve 1 clove for finishing oil if you want stronger garlic punch.

ingredients.label: Fresh thyme leaves (or dried thyme)
notes: Or 1/2 tsp dried.

ingredients.label: Unsalted butter, melted (optional finish)
notes: For a richer finish; omit for dairy-light. Use olive oil instead.

steps.title: Mix the dough
instructions: In a mixing bowl, whisk flour, yeast, sugar, and salt.
Add yogurt, warm water, olive oil, and minced/grated garlic.
Stir until a shaggy dough forms. If very sticky, add flour 1 tbsp at a time; if dry, add water 1 tbsp at a time.
checks: Dough comes together into a soft ball

steps.title: Knead briefly and rest
instructions: Turn dough onto a lightly floured surface and knead 2–3 minutes until smoother.
Place back in the bowl, cover, and let rest 20 minutes (this relaxes gluten and helps rolling).
If you have time, rest 30–45 minutes for slightly puffier flatbreads.

steps.title: Divide and shape
instructions: Roll each piece into a ball, then roll into a 6–7 inch round (about 1/4 inch thick).
Lightly flour as needed to prevent sticking.
checks: Rounds are even thickness for consistent cooking

steps.title: Cook in a hot skillet
instructions: Heat a skillet over medium-high heat until hot.
Cook one flatbread at a time: 60–90 seconds on the first side until bubbles form and brown spots appear.
Remove to a plate and cover with a towel to keep soft while you cook the rest.
checks: Flatbread is cooked through but still soft

steps.title: Finish with herb-garlic oil (optional) and serve
instructions: Optional finish: brush warm flatbreads with olive oil or melted butter.
Sprinkle with chopped herbs (parsley/chives/thyme) and a pinch of salt.
Serve warm with dips, soups, or as a wrap.

refrigerator.guidance: Cool completely and store airtight. Reheat in a dry skillet 30–60 seconds per side.

freezer.guidance: Freeze flatbreads with parchment between. Reheat from frozen in a skillet or toaster oven.

best: Dry skillet on medium 30–60 seconds per side

batchNotes: Cooked flatbreads hold well for a few hours wrapped in a clean towel.
If doubling, keep cooked breads warm in a low oven (200°F) loosely covered.
leftovers: Cut into wedges and toast for croutons/chips
Use as quick pizza base (broil briefly with toppings)

summary: Soft skillet herb flatbread with garlic and olive oil—quick to make, tear-and-share friendly, and perfect with Herb & Garlic mains and dips.

handling: Cook flatbreads fully; avoid raw dough in the center.
Cool before sealing airtight to prevent condensation and sogginess.

notes: Replace yeast + sugar with 2 tsp baking powder.
Skip long rest; rest 10 minutes to hydrate flour, then roll and cook.
Texture becomes more like quick naan—still excellent.
Replace 1 cup all-purpose flour with 1 cup whole wheat flour.
Add 1–2 tbsp extra water if dough feels dry.
Replace yogurt with unsweetened dairy-free yogurt OR 3/4 cup warm water + 1 tbsp lemon juice.
Omit butter finish; use olive oil.
Serve with a plate of olive oil + lemon juice + salt + chopped herbs for dipping.
Add grated garlic to the oil for a stronger garlic finish.
